ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers liquid green tea; no specific grapefruit-flavoured variant exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024. Closest defensible match used and documented.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 ml (350 g), equivalent to a typical takeaway cup of freshly made grapefruit green tea served in Singapore bubble tea or specialty tea shops.

nutrient_source.title: Grapefruit Green Tea (Less Sugar) — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Grapefruit Green Tea (Less Sugar), record ID D08010032. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical HPB entry at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for grapefruit green tea (less sugar) as a composite beverage. GI estimated at 52 based on two analogous approved-source values: (1) grapefruit juice GI ~48 (University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2008) and (2) sugar-sweetened green tea GI ~55 (University of Sydney GI Database). A blended estimate of 52 is used, reflecting the dominant carbohydrate source (sugar at 8.26g/100g) in a liquid matrix with negligible fibre. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (52) × carbohydrate per serving (29.0g for 350g serving) / 100 = 15.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar content (8.26g/100g) in a liquid matrix with negligible fibre; grapefruit–drug interaction risk
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium is very low (1mg/100g), the 8.26g/100g sugar content in this beverage independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines on sugar-sweetened beverages; patients on antihypertensive medications should also be aware that grapefruit compounds (furanocoumarins) inhibit CYP3A4 and may potentiate calcium channel blockers and other antihypertensives — consult prescriber before regular consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.26g sugar/100g, negligible fibre (0.03g/100g), and liquid carbohydrate matrix causing rapid glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage contains 8.26g of sugar per 100g with virtually no fibre, classifying it as a sugar-sweetened beverage; liquid sugars are absorbed rapidly and cause acute postprandial glucose spikes, making this unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes per ADA 2023 Standards of Care — an unsweetened (zero-sugar) version should be requested if consumption is desired.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar load (8.26g/100g) with no saturated fat or sodium concern; grapefruit–statin interaction risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The fat and sodium profile of this beverage is favourable for cardiovascular health (0g fat, 1mg sodium/100g); however, the added sugar content (8.26g/100g) contributes to excess caloric intake and triglyceride elevation with regular consumption, and patients on statins or other CYP3A4-metabolised cardiovascular medications must avoid grapefruit-containing products — consult prescriber.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Green tea catechins (EGCG) with anti-inflammatory properties; grapefruit–medication interaction risk
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Green tea provides catechins (EGCG) with established anti-inflammatory properties relevant to rheumatoid arthritis management; however, grapefruit compounds may interact with methotrexate and other RA medications via CYP enzyme inhibition — consult prescriber before regular consumption, and prefer an unsweetened version to minimise pro-inflammatory sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grapefruit Green Tea (Less Sugar) is a freshly prepared specialty tea beverage popular in Singapore's bubble tea and specialty drink shops. It is made by brewing green tea and infusing it with grapefruit juice, then sweetening with a reduced amount of sugar compared to the standard version. The drink has a light, slightly tart citrus flavour from the grapefruit, balanced by the mild grassy notes of green tea, with a thin, watery texture typical of fruit-infused teas. Per 100g, it provides 43 kcal, 8.26g carbohydrate (all from sugar), 0.01g protein, 0g fat, 0.03g dietary fibre, and 1mg sodium. Despite the 'less sugar' label, the sugar content remains at 8.26g/100g, classifying it as a sugar-sweetened beverage. This is clinically significant: the liquid sugar matrix with negligible fibre results in rapid glucose absorption, making it unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing hypertension. Grapefruit compounds (furanocoumarins) are known CYP3A4 inhibitors and may interact with antihypertensives, statins, methotrexate, and other medications. Green tea contributes catechins (EGCG) with anti-inflammatory properties. The beverage is plant-based, vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, and very low in sodium. It is commonly consumed as a breakfast or snack drink in Singapore. Customisable toppings such as pearls or jelly may significantly alter the caloric and carbohydrate profile.
common_user_queries: Can diabetics drink grapefruit green tea?
How many calories in grapefruit green tea less sugar?
Is grapefruit green tea suitable for hypertension?
Does grapefruit tea interact with medications?
What is the sugar content of less sugar grapefruit green tea?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (record D08010032) at confidence 0.95. This is an exact match from the primary approved regional source for Singapore foods. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database approved sources, clearly documented.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (D08010032); this is a water-based tea beverage containing no fat by formulation — confirmed zero. saturated_fat_g and cholesterol_mg are confirmed zero consistent with a fat-free beverage. protein_g is 0.01g (trace, not zero). monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid breakdown for this specific freshly prepared grapefruit green tea (less sugar) formulation is not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. GI value of 52 is an analogous estimate based on grapefruit juice GI (~48) and sugar-sweetened green tea GI (~55) from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). Customisable toppings (pearls, jelly, etc.) are not included in the per_100g values and would substantially alter caloric and carbohydrate content. Grapefruit furanocoumarins are CYP3A4 inhibitors — clinically relevant drug interaction warning applies.
